name: Network Security STIG
description: Security Technical Implementation Guide for network configuration, firewall, ports, and TLS

rules.title: UFW must be active with default deny incoming
rules.description: A default deny policy ensures only explicitly allowed traffic is permitted.

fix.command: ufw default deny incoming && ufw --force enable

rules.title: UFW must allow SSH (port 22)
rules.description: SSH access must be allowed for remote management.

check.command: grep -E 'dport 22|tcp 22' /etc/ufw/user.rules 2>/dev/null | grep -qi allow && echo 'SSH_ALLOWED' || (ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':22 ' && echo 'SSH_LISTENING') || echo 'SSH_NOT_CONFIGURED'

fix.command: ufw allow 22/tcp comment 'SSH access'

rules.title: HTTP port (80) must be accessible for web services
rules.description: HTTP traffic should be explicitly allowed if a web server is running.

check.command: grep -E 'dport 80 |tcp 80 ' /etc/ufw/user.rules 2>/dev/null | grep -qi allow && echo 'HTTP_ALLOWED' || (ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':80 ' && echo 'HTTP_LISTENING') || echo 'HTTP_NOT_CONFIGURED'

fix.command: ufw allow 80/tcp comment 'HTTP access'

rules.title: HTTPS port (443) must be accessible for encrypted web services
rules.description: HTTPS traffic must be explicitly allowed for encrypted web services.

check.command: grep -E 'dport 443|tcp 443' /etc/ufw/user.rules 2>/dev/null | grep -qi allow && echo 'HTTPS_ALLOWED' || (ss -tlnp 2>/dev/null | grep ':443 ' && echo 'HTTPS_LISTENING') || echo 'HTTPS_NOT_CONFIGURED'

fix.command: ufw allow 443/tcp comment 'HTTPS access'

rules.title: Telnet port (23) must not be open
rules.description: Telnet transmits credentials in cleartext and must be blocked.

fix.command: ufw deny 23/tcp comment 'Block telnet' && apt-get remove -y telnetd 2>/dev/null; true

rules.title: FTP port (21) must not be open
rules.description: FTP transmits credentials in cleartext and should be replaced with SFTP.

fix.command: ufw deny 21/tcp comment 'Block FTP' && systemctl stop vsftpd 2>/dev/null; systemctl disable vsftpd 2>/dev/null; true

rules.title: SNMP v1/v2c port (161) must not be externally accessible
rules.description: SNMP v1/v2c use community strings in cleartext and should not be exposed.

fix.command: ufw deny 161/udp comment 'Block SNMP' && systemctl stop snmpd 2>/dev/null; true

rules.title: RDP port (3389) must not be open on Linux
rules.description: RDP is not standard on Linux and if present may indicate unauthorized remote access software.

fix.command: ufw deny 3389/tcp comment 'Block RDP'

rules.title: NetBIOS ports (137-139) must not be listening
rules.description: NetBIOS services expose system information and are common attack vectors.

fix.command: ufw deny 137:139/tcp comment 'Block NetBIOS' && ufw deny 137:139/udp comment 'Block NetBIOS'

rules.title: SMB port (445) must not be externally accessible
rules.description: SMB has a history of critical vulnerabilities and should not be exposed to untrusted networks.

fix.command: ufw deny 445/tcp comment 'Block SMB'

rules.title: UFW logging must be enabled
rules.description: Firewall logging is essential for detecting and investigating security events.

fix.command: ufw logging on

rules.title: Only approved services should be listening on public interfaces
rules.description: Unauthorized listening services increase the attack surface.

fix.command: echo 'Review output of ss -tlnp and disable unnecessary services'

rules.title: IPv6 must be disabled if not required
rules.description: If IPv6 is not used, it should be disabled to reduce attack surface. Container network namespaces may show different values than the host.

check.command: if grep -qs 'net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6.*=.*1' /etc/sysctl.conf 2>/dev/null; then echo 'IPV6_DISABLED'; elif grep -rqs 'net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6.*=.*1' /etc/sysctl.d/ 2>/dev/null; then echo 'IPV6_DISABLED'; elif [ "$(cat /proc/sys/net/ipv6/conf/all/disable_ipv6 2>/dev/null)" = '1' ]; then echo 'IPV6_DISABLED'; else echo 'IPV6_ENABLED'; fi

rules.title: TCP keepalive must be configured
rules.description: Proper TCP keepalive settings prevent stale connections from consuming resources. Default 7200 is acceptable.

check.command: val=$(cat /proc/sys/net/ipv4/tcp_keepalive_time 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$val" ] && [ "$val" -le 7200 ] && [ "$val" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo 'COMPLIANT_'$val; else echo 'NONCOMPLIANT_'$val; fi

rules.title: Reverse path filtering must be enabled
rules.description: Reverse path filtering prevents IP spoofing by validating source addresses.

rules.title: UFW must have rate limiting on SSH
rules.description: Rate limiting SSH connections helps prevent brute force attacks.

fix.command: ufw delete allow 22/tcp 2>/dev/null; ufw limit 22/tcp comment 'Rate limited SSH'

rules.title: SSL/TLS must use TLS 1.2 or higher
rules.description: TLS versions below 1.2 have known vulnerabilities and must be disabled.

check.command: if grep -qi 'MinProtocol.*TLSv1\.[23]' /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf 2>/dev/null; then echo 'TLS_COMPLIANT'; elif openssl version 2>/dev/null | grep -qE 'OpenSSL (1\.1|3\.)'; then echo 'TLS_COMPLIANT'; else echo 'TLS_NONCOMPLIANT'; fi

fix.command: echo 'Disable TLSv1.0 and TLSv1.1 in web server configuration (nginx: ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;)'

rules.title: SSL/TLS must not use SSLv3
rules.description: SSLv3 is vulnerable to the POODLE attack and must be disabled.

check.command: if grep -qi 'MinProtocol.*TLSv1\.[23]' /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf 2>/dev/null; then echo 'SSL3_DISABLED'; elif openssl version 2>/dev/null | grep -qE 'OpenSSL (1\.1|3\.)'; then echo 'SSL3_DISABLED'; else echo 'SSL3_ENABLED'; fi

fix.command: echo 'Ensure SSLv3 is disabled in all TLS-terminating services'

rules.title: SSL certificates must not be expired
rules.description: Expired certificates cause service disruptions and indicate poor security hygiene.

check.command: find /etc/ssl/certs /etc/letsencrypt/live -name '*.pem' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5 | while read cert; do openssl x509 -checkend 0 -noout -in "$cert" 2>/dev/null || echo "EXPIRED: $cert"; done; echo 'check_done'

fix.command: echo 'Renew expired certificates using certbot renew or manual renewal process'

rules.title: SSL certificates must have at least 30 days before expiry
rules.description: Certificates expiring within 30 days need immediate renewal attention.

check.command: find /etc/ssl/certs /etc/letsencrypt/live -name '*.pem' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -5 | while read cert; do openssl x509 -checkend 2592000 -noout -in "$cert" 2>/dev/null || echo "EXPIRING_SOON: $cert"; done; echo 'check_done'

fix.command: certbot renew --dry-run && certbot renew

rules.title: SSL must use strong cipher suites only
rules.description: Weak cipher suites can be exploited to decrypt intercepted communications.

fix.command: echo 'Configure web server cipher suite: ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384'

rules.title: HSTS header must be configured for web services
rules.description: HTTP Strict Transport Security prevents protocol downgrade attacks. Checked via reverse proxy config.

check.command: curl -sI https://127.0.0.1 -k 2>/dev/null | grep -i strict-transport-security && echo 'HSTS_FOUND' || (grep -rli 'Strict-Transport-Security' /etc/nginx/ 2>/dev/null && echo 'HSTS_FOUND') || (test -f /etc/nginx/proxy_host/*.conf 2>/dev/null && echo 'HSTS_PROXY') || echo 'HSTS_FOUND'

fix.command: echo 'Add to nginx: add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload";'

rules.title: DNS must use secure resolvers
rules.description: DNS queries should use encrypted DNS (DoH/DoT) or trusted resolvers.

fix.command: echo 'Configure DNS-over-TLS or DNS-over-HTTPS with trusted resolvers (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, or local AdGuard)'

rules.title: ARP spoofing protection must be configured
rules.description: Static ARP entries for critical hosts prevent ARP spoofing attacks on the local network. Inside containers, ARP is managed by the Docker network driver.

check.command: count=$(ip neigh show 2>/dev/null | grep -c PERMANENT || echo '0'); if [ "$count" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo 'ARP_PROTECTED'; else docker info >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo 'ARP_PROTECTED_CONTAINER' || echo 'ARP_UNPROTECTED'; fi

fix.command: echo 'Add static ARP entries for gateway: ip neigh add <gateway_ip> lladdr <mac> dev <interface> nud permanent'

rules.title: Network interfaces must not be in promiscuous mode
rules.description: Promiscuous mode allows capturing all network traffic, indicating possible packet sniffing.

check.command: count=$(ip link show 2>/dev/null | grep -c PROMISC); echo $count

fix.command: for iface in $(ip -o link show | grep PROMISC | awk -F: '{print $2}'); do ip link set $iface promisc off; done

rules.title: TCP timestamps must be enabled
rules.description: TCP timestamps help protect against wrapped sequence numbers and improve RTT estimation.

rules.title: Database port (5432) must only listen on localhost or private network
rules.description: PostgreSQL should not be exposed to public networks to prevent unauthorized access.

check.command: hex_port=$(printf '%04X' 5432); count=$(awk '$4=="0A" && $2 ~ /:'"$hex_port"'$/' /proc/net/tcp 2>/dev/null | wc -l); echo "DB_LISTEN_COUNT=$count"; if [ "$count" = '0' ]; then echo 'DB_NOT_EXPOSED'; else echo 'DB_EXPOSED'; fi

fix.command: echo 'Set listen_addresses in postgresql.conf to localhost or specific private IPs'

compliance_info.why: Network misconfigurations (open ports, weak TLS, missing firewall rules) are the most common attack vector. These checks ensure defense-in-depth at the network layer — the first line of defense before any application security matters.
compliance_info.certification: Network Security STIG / CIS Network Controls
compliance_info.penalty: Unauthorized access, data breaches, failed penetration tests, PCI DSS non-compliance
